1.1: No existing data will be reused. All data will be newly collected specifically for this research.

The project will generate the following types of data: [type 1] (format, approximately [size]), [type 2] (format, approximately [size]), [type 3] (format, approximately [size]). The total volume of all data collected during the project is estimated to be no more than [X] GB.

The research will produce: survey responses from approximately [N] participants collected through online questionnaires, resulting in tabular data of approximately [X] MB; interview recordings ([N] semi-structured interviews of [X] minutes each), approximately [X] GB of audio files; transcriptions and coded data from qualitative analysis.
The project will generate measurement data from [instrument/method]: approximately [N] samples, resulting in approximately [X] GB of data in [format] format.

During the project, the following main types of data will be generated: raw experimental data from laboratory instruments; processed and analysed data derived from raw measurements; working data including digital laboratory notebooks and intermediate results; secondary data resulting from the interpretation and statistical analysis of raw data.
1.2: The formats of the saved files can be divided into two types: formats specific to laboratory equipment software (e.g., [proprietary format 1], [proprietary format 2]), and commonly used formats including text (.txt, .csv), tabular (.csv, .xlsx), and graphical (.tiff, .png) formats. The total volume of all files will not exceed [X] GB.

Data will be stored in the following open formats: .csv for tabular data, .txt for transcriptions and documentation, .py for analysis scripts, .pdf for final reports, .tiff for microscopy images, .png for figures and visualizations. Working data will be maintained in [format] during active analysis. Upon project completion, all datasets intended for repository deposit will be converted to open formats to ensure long-term accessibility.

To ensure the broadest possible access to research data, data intended for deposition in the repository, both raw and analysed, will be saved in open file formats. This will only be done when no information will be lost and when researchers in this field of science commonly use such file formats.

Data will be stored in formats specific to the laboratory equipment software as well as in commonly used open formats (.csv, .txt, .tiff). All data selected for long-term archiving and sharing will be deposited in suitable open formats.
2.1: All data deposited in the MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalog (Bridge of Data) will be described using attributes compatible with general metadata standards, such as DataCite. Metadata descriptions will be stored in JSON-LD format and include, whenever possible, Persistent Identifiers (PID), such as ORCID or GRID.

A comprehensive README.txt file will accompany each dataset, containing: description of research methodology, explanation of all variables with units of measurement and value ranges, data collection procedures, structure of files and folders, software requirements for data access, known limitations or quality issues, and contact information for data-related inquiries.

The collected files will have standardized names enabling rapid identification of the data contained therein. The folder structure will be organized as follows: /raw_data, /processed_data, /scripts, /results, /documentation. File naming convention: YYYY-MM-DD_[experimentID]_[sampleID].[extension].

Due to the fact that the MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalog (Bridge of Data) repository is indexed in the Web of Science Data Citation Index, the metadata descriptions for project datasets will also be findable in this well-known service from Clarivate Analytics.

Raw data, due to the specifics of laboratory equipment software, may have different sets of metadata, which will not be modified in any way. The most important measure for keeping consistency in the recording of individual samples and the precise tracking of each stage of the research process will be laboratory notebooks, both in paper and digital versions.
2.2: Data quality will be ensured through: double-entry verification for all data records by two independent researchers, automated validation scripts checking for out-of-range values and missing data, regular calibration of measurement equipment according to manufacturer protocols with calibration records maintained, and laboratory notebooks kept meticulously in both paper and digital versions.

To ensure the quality of data obtained in the laboratory, standard procedures and good laboratory practices will be applied, primarily through meticulous maintenance of laboratory notebooks in both paper and digital versions. Each measurement will be verified and, in case of any doubts regarding its correctness, repeated.

The data will be collected in accordance with applicable protocols and scientific standards. Any statistical corrections will be consulted with a qualified statistician. All deviations from standard procedures will be documented in the laboratory notebook.
3.1: Data will be stored on Gdansk Tech institutional network drives with automated daily backups managed by the university IT department. Working copies will be maintained on encrypted, password-protected researcher computers. Additional weekly backups will be made to an encrypted external hard drive stored off-campus in a secure location.

During the project, data will be stored on: the primary researcher's password-protected workstation at Gdansk University of Technology; institutional OneDrive with daily automated synchronization (Office 365 A1 for Faculty license, managed by Gdansk Tech IT Services Center); weekly manual backups to an encrypted external hard drive stored off-site.

All data recorded on laboratory computers will be transferred automatically or manually to the institutional network-attached storage (NAS) server, which automatically backs up all files. Additionally, data under active analysis on team members' computers will be secured through automatic backups performed via institutional OneDrive (Office 365 A1 for Faculty license).

A 3-2-1 backup strategy will be applied: 3 copies of all data, stored on 2 different types of media, with 1 copy kept off-site. Specifically: primary copy on researcher workstation, second copy on institutional OneDrive (daily automated sync), third copy on encrypted external hard drive stored at a separate secure location (weekly manual backup).
3.2: All data will be stored on password-protected and encrypted devices. Access will be restricted to authorized project members only. Cloud storage will be secured through institutional IT security protocols, including two-factor authentication. The security of the data storage system will be the responsibility of the Gdansk Tech IT Services Center.

No research data containing personal information requiring additional security measures will be collected or processed during the project. Standard methods will be applied to protect data from leaks and unauthorized use and to ensure data integrity. Access to all collected data and the results of its analysis will be exclusively for the principal investigator and project team members designated by them.

Team members will be responsible for systematically changing security passwords and installing the latest software updates. Only the principal investigator and specifically designated project members have access to all data. Technical staff and students participating only in implementing a part of the project do not have access to data discs.
4.1: No personal data will be collected during the project. The research does not involve human participants and does not require ethics committee approval.

This research does not require ethics committee approval as determined by institutional regulations. The study involves anonymous online surveys without collection of identifiable personal data or sensitive information. Participants will be informed about the voluntary nature of participation and the anonymous character of data collection.

The study received approval from the [name of] Ethics Committee (approval number [X] dated [date]). All participants provided written informed consent prior to participation. Personal data processing complies with GDPR regulations.

Personal data processing complies with GDPR regulations. Legal basis: informed consent obtained from all participants. Personal identifiers will be separated from research data immediately upon collection and replaced with pseudonymized codes. Only aggregated or fully anonymized data will be published. Identifiable personal information will be stored separately on encrypted drives, accessible only to the principal investigator, and destroyed [X] years after project completion.

Images and other data collected from a medical database will be subject to anonymization at the point of selection, in accordance with GDPR provisions. In subsequent stages of the project, no personal data will be collected or processed. No data enabling identification of individuals will be published or shared.
4.2: The right to manage and use both the data and any derivatives based on them belongs to the Gdansk University of Technology under the Intellectual Property Regulations adopted by the Gdansk Tech Senate (Resolution No. 117/2021/XXV of 19 May 2021, https://link.pg.edu.pl/GdanskTech_intprop). Whenever possible, dissemination of project deliverables will be carried out using open-access channels, e.g. under Creative Commons licenses.

Data management will comply with the Intellectual Property Regulations of Gdansk University of Technology (https://link.pg.edu.pl/GdanskTech_intprop). In the case of analyses conducted outside the university, a commercial agreement will be signed with the contractor, under which Gdansk Tech will retain exclusive rights to the research results.

The ownership and management of any intellectual property relating to the project remain in the rights of the Gdansk University of Technology and the research team members accordingly to the Polish law and institutional regulations (Resolution of the Senate of the Gdansk University of Technology No. 117/2021/XXV of 19 May 2021, https://link.pg.edu.pl/GdanskTech_intprop).

Ultimately, all raw data will be made available under the CC0 license, while data representing the analysis and interpretation of laboratory results will be under the CC BY license. All metadata describing datasets in the MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalog (Bridge of Data) will always be available without any restrictions (CC0). Note: the choice of CC0 for raw data should be confirmed with the Gdansk Tech Legal Department given the intellectual property regulations.
5.1: The primary place for sharing research data will be the institutional repository MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalog (Bridge of Data). Selected data will be divided into datasets so that each dataset will serve as the basis for one article. The datasets will be made available at the time of the article's publication.

Data will be shared in accordance with the publication dates of the articles derived from this data. The data will be deposited in the MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalog (Bridge of Data), a CoreTrustSeal certified repository provided by Gdansk University of Technology, at the latest when articles based on these data are published.
Data will be published under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC BY 4.0) license, allowing reuse with attribution requirement.

An embargo of [X] months is requested to enable completion of data analysis and submission of manuscripts for peer review. Data will be made publicly available immediately upon publication acceptance. Embargo end date: [specific date, e.g. December 31, 2026]. If the publication process exceeds the project's duration, the data will be deposited in the repository and the metadata will be published, with access to the data itself embargoed until the article is published.
Data will be openly accessible immediately upon deposition with no embargo period. Anyone can freely download and use the data under CC BY 4.0 license terms.
No data containing information enabling the identification of individuals will be shared. Only aggregated and fully anonymized data will be made available in the repository.
5.2: The MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalog (Bridge of Data) will be the primary repository for data long-term preservation. Data deposited there will be automatically categorised for long-term storage without an expiration date. The repository is CoreTrustSeal certified -- the only certified research data repository in Poland -- which confirms its trustworthiness and sustainability.

The MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalog (Bridge of Data) ensures FAIR principles: Findable (comprehensive metadata, persistent DOI, indexed in Web of Science Data Citation Index), Accessible (open web interface, clear licensing), Interoperable (DataCite metadata standard, open formats), Reusable (detailed documentation in README, explicit license).

All datasets deposited in the MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalog (Bridge of Data) repository automatically receive DOI identifiers at the time of their publication, ensuring that datasets are easily traceable and citable.

Moreover, all data not selected for sharing will be stored by the PI for at least 10 years after the project is finished, and access to them will be possible upon direct request with the PI's consent.
5.3: To ensure the broadest possible access to data, data intended for deposition in the repository, both raw and analysed, will be saved in open file formats. This will only be done when no information will be lost and when researchers in this field of science commonly use such file formats. Data in proprietary formats will be provided upon direct request.
5.4: All datasets deposited in the MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalog (Bridge of Data) repository automatically receive DOI identifiers at the time of their publication, to ensure that datasets are easily traceable and citable.
6.1: The principal investigator will be responsible for collecting and storing data, as well as for its substantive quality and compliance with FAIR principles. Technical support in data security and backups is available from the Gdansk Tech IT Services Center (CUI).

The Principal Investigator, [Full Name], [Position], Gdansk University of Technology, will be responsible for overall data management throughout the project and for depositing data in the repository upon completion. Technical support is available from the Open Science Competence Center (openaccess@pg.edu.pl) for DMP verification and metadata preparation for datasets in the MOST Wiedzy repository.

Support in various aspects of research data management will be provided by Gdansk Tech units, including the Open Science Competence Center (CKON) (verification of DMP, metadata for datasets in the repository, contact: openaccess@pg.edu.pl) and the IT Services Center (CUI) (data security, backups, network storage).
The proper abbreviation for our university is Gdansk Tech (full name: Gdansk University of Technology). Please use this abbreviation consistently throughout the document.
6.2: Managing data in compliance with FAIR principles does not require additional financial resources, as Gdansk Tech provides both the institutional NAS server and OneDrive service (Office 365 A1 for Faculty). Similarly, the MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalog (Bridge of Data) repository has institutional funding, and depositing data and their long-term archiving are free of charge for Gdansk Tech researchers.

No additional financial costs are anticipated for data management. MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalog (Bridge of Data) repository services are provided free of charge to Gdansk Tech researchers. Personnel time for data curation and documentation (approximately [X] working days) is included in the project personnel budget.

The costs of research data management consist primarily of personnel time: approximately [X] working days for data preparation, documentation, quality control, and deposit in the repository. These costs are included in the project budget under personnel costs. No additional financial expenditure is required, as all storage and repository services are provided free of charge by Gdansk Tech.
